basics.name: Ronald E. Straight
basics.label: Senior Software Engineer
basics.summary: I bring decades of experience in high-performance backend engineering, from C and Perl to modern Golang and Kubernetes-native Java and Rust microservices. AI coding agents are good collaborators, but they still need someone to tell them when they're about to rimraf something important — with agentic coding you get my unique human experience plus a capable team for the tedious parts. Work is where I want to swim with my fellow clever rubber ducks.

work.name: Man and Woman, Living Together
highlights: Making bread from scratch every other day or so, and sometimes pepperoni rolls.
Ongoing hacking projects with my artificial friends, including running two multiplayer games in my home server's Kubernetes cluster.

work.name: Synacor
highlights: Led the architecture and implementation of converting the Zimbra email/collaboration platform to a multi-tenant Kubernetes-native service, utilizing Golang and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure.
Contributed to core Zimbra engineering, working on legacy Java codebase with extensive use of remote debuggers.
Leveraged Golang and Kubernetes expertise to migrate several hundred million mailboxes from bare-metal servers to Zimbra on Kubernetes.

work.name: Adblade / Solve Media
highlights: Built a high-availability RESTful Data Management Platform (DMP) using Java/Netty, supporting sub-millisecond responses at scale.
Developed custom dispatching annotations and supported multiple HTTP methods for consumer targeting resources stored in Redis.
Ported a legacy image conversion REST server from Ruby to Java and migrated a Solaris data center environment to AWS.

work.name: Kanoodle / Pulse360 / Seevast
highlights: Contributed Oracle-specific enhancements upstream to the DBIx::Class open-source ORM framework while developing high-traffic Perl ad-serving applications.
Performed Oracle DBA duties including schema review and analytic query development.
Built 'RoboDeploy', a multi-threaded parallel deployment system serving over 100 production servers.

work.name: Netsville
highlights: Co-founded Netsville and developed 'Hazel,' one of the first e-commerce checkout systems, written in C with a templating language (HZML).

projects.name: Logodal
projects.description: Multiplayer whimsical word game running on a kind cluster in my bedroom. In active development.
highlights: Developed a custom wordlist server in Rust to handle building lexicons and their rules for any language.
Configured networking for public access to the local Kubernetes cluster at logodal.com.
Implemented real-time gameplay mechanics using Perl, plus simple intelligent dummy players.

projects.name: Card Shoggoths
projects.description: Multiplayer poker game with a bit of cosmic horror. In active development.
highlights: Implemented the game server in Golang with a minimal HTML/CSS frontend.
Configured networking for public access to the local Kubernetes cluster at poker.fazigu.org.
